enrichment.short_description: Lavu is a cloud-based iPad point-of-sale system for restaurants with AI-driven financial and operations tools.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Lavu is an iPad restaurant POS with AI agents for finance, marketing, menu, labor, and inventory management.

features.title: iPad Point of Sale
features.description: Cloud-based POS software built to run on iPad devices for restaurant front-of-house operations.

features.title: Kitchen Display System
features.description: Digital kitchen display system (KDS) for routing and tracking orders in the kitchen.

features.title: Marty AI agents
features.description: Six AI agents covering marketing, menu, labor, inventory, server, and finance tasks.

features.title: Payment processing
features.description: Built-in payment processing with contactless payment options.

features.title: Online ordering
features.description: Online ordering capability integrated with the POS platform.

features.title: Payroll management
features.description: Payroll management functionality offered as part of the platform.

features.title: Cross-POS financial reporting
features.description: Financial insight reports (e.g. 'Morning Deposit') that can connect to other POS systems including Toast, Square, Clover, and Aloha.

pros: Offers AI-driven financial insights (Marty AI, six specialized agents)
Can connect to and report on data from other POS systems (Toast, Square, Clover, Aloha)
Bundles payroll and marketing automation alongside POS
Includes built-in kitchen display and online ordering features
cons: Pricing is not published on the website and requires contacting sales or requesting a demo
Core POS runs on iPad hardware, requiring Apple devices
No free trial or free plan is stated on the site

faqs.question: What is Lavu?
faqs.answer: Lavu is a cloud-based restaurant POS system that runs on iPad and includes AI-powered financial and operations tools.

faqs.question: Does Lavu offer AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, Lavu includes 'Marty AI' with six agents covering marketing, menu, labor, inventory, server, and finance.

faqs.question: What other POS systems can Lavu connect to?
faqs.answer: Lavu's site mentions compatibility with Toast, Square, Clover, and Aloha for financial reporting.

faqs.question: Is Lavu's pricing publicly listed?
faqs.answer: No, the website does not list specific pricing and directs visitors to buy directly, request a demo, or call sales.

faqs.question: What hardware does Lavu run on?
faqs.answer: Lavu's POS is primarily built for iPad.

use_cases.title: Full-service restaurant POS
use_cases.description: Running front-of-house ordering and payments on iPad hardware.

use_cases.title: Cross-platform financial insight
use_cases.description: Getting AI-generated financial reports even when using a different existing POS system.

use_cases.title: Automated marketing and labor tools
use_cases.description: Using AI agents to help with marketing campaigns, menu decisions, and labor management.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Lavu?
paragraphs: Lavu is a cloud-based restaurant point-of-sale system built primarily for iPad, offering ordering, kitchen display, payments, and payroll tools.

The platform includes 'Marty AI,' a set of six AI agents focused on marketing, menu, labor, inventory, server, and finance tasks, and can generate financial reports even when connected to other POS systems like Toast, Square, Clover, or Aloha.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Lavu is for
paragraphs: Lavu is aimed at restaurant owners and operators who want an iPad-based POS combined with AI-assisted financial reporting, marketing automation, and payroll management.

enrichment.short_description: GoTab is a POS and mobile order-and-pay platform for restaurants, breweries, and hospitality venues.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: GoTab offers POS, QR mobile ordering, and kitchen display tools for restaurants and breweries from $15/month.

features.title: Point of sale system
features.description: Core POS system for order entry and payment processing.

features.title: Mobile order & payment
features.description: QR-code based mobile ordering and payment for guests.

features.title: Kitchen Display System
features.description: Digital KDS available as an add-on for order routing in the kitchen.

features.title: Handheld / Pocket POS
features.description: Handheld POS devices for tableside or counter service.

features.title: Self-ordering kiosks
features.description: Self-service ordering kiosks available as an add-on.

features.title: Loyalty & marketing tools
features.description: Loyalty and marketing features available as an add-on on Pro and Sync plans.

features.title: Gift cards, memberships & subscriptions
features.description: Support for gift cards, memberships, and subscription offerings.

features.title: Manager dashboard
features.description: Dashboard for venue managers to oversee operations.

pros: Low-cost entry point with a $15/month Basic plan
Runs on Android, iOS, and Windows tablets, plus web access
Supports English and Spanish
Higher tiers bundle 24/7 support, accounting integration, and Easy Tab at no extra add-on cost
Company states it processes over $1B in annual transactions
cons: Many features (KDS, kiosk, extra terminals, loyalty, fraud protection) are paid add-ons on the Basic and Pro plans
Full feature set (on-premise server, fraud protection, 24/7 support) requires the $229/month Sync plan
Card processing starts at 2.40% + $0.15, with additional surcharges for Amex, JCB, corporate cards, and international transactions
Setup fees are custom-quoted rather than published

faqs.question: What is GoTab?
faqs.answer: GoTab is a POS and mobile order-and-pay platform used by restaurants, breweries, food halls, hotels, and similar venues.

faqs.question: How much does GoTab cost?
faqs.answer: GoTab offers Basic ($15/month), Pro ($99/month), and Sync ($229/month) plans, with several features available as paid add-ons.

faqs.question: What payment processing rate does GoTab charge?
faqs.answer: GoTab states card processing starts at 2.40% + $0.15 for in-person transactions, with extra surcharges for Amex, JCB, corporate cards, and international payments.

faqs.question: What languages does GoTab support?
faqs.answer: GoTab's site states support for English and Spanish.

faqs.question: What devices does GoTab run on?
faqs.answer: GoTab supports Android and iOS devices as well as Windows tablets, plus web access.

use_cases.title: QR mobile ordering for breweries and food halls
use_cases.description: Letting guests order and pay via QR code at the table or counter.

use_cases.title: Multi-location venue management
use_cases.description: Managing POS operations across multiple locations using the Pro or Sync plan add-on.

use_cases.title: Loyalty and membership programs
use_cases.description: Running gift card, membership, and loyalty programs through the platform.
use_cases.audience: Hospitality venues focused on repeat guests

features: Additional POS terminal, KDS, self-ordering kiosk, Easy Tab, 24/7 support, and accounting integration available as paid add-ons
Optional add-ons: on-premise server (+$75/mo), loyalty & memberships (+$35/mo), fraud protection (+$35/mo), multi-location management (+$50/mo)
Includes 24/7 support, accounting integration, and fraud protection
Optional add-ons: loyalty & memberships (+$35/mo), multi-location management (+$50/mo)

long_form_blocks.heading: What is GoTab?
paragraphs: GoTab is a POS and mobile order-and-pay platform for restaurants, breweries, food halls, hotels, and similar venues, combining a core point-of-sale system with QR-code mobile ordering.

Pricing starts at $15/month for a Basic plan, with Pro ($99/month) and Sync ($229/month) tiers adding features like 24/7 support, accounting integration, and on-premise server capability; many features such as kitchen display, kiosks, and loyalty tools are available as paid add-ons.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who GoTab is for
paragraphs: GoTab is aimed at restaurants, breweries, food halls, and hospitality venues that want mobile QR ordering alongside traditional POS, with the ability to add features like kiosks, loyalty programs, and multi-location management as needed.

name: Upserve
skip_reason: upserve.com no longer operates as an independent site; it 301-redirects to https://www.lightspeedhq.com/upserve/, a landing page on Lightspeed's domain. The Upserve product name is still referenced but is now folded entirely into Lightspeed's site and portfolio rather than having its own operating website, so there isn't a standalone official source to verify current pricing, features, or company facts against.

enrichment.short_description: Restaurant back-office software that automates invoice processing, food cost tracking, and daily P&L reporting.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: MarginEdge automates restaurant invoice processing, food costing, and daily P&L reporting for independent and multi-unit operators.

features.title: Automated Invoice Processing
features.description: Digitizes and processes vendor invoices automatically; MarginEdge has processed more than 5.0 million invoices to date.

features.title: Daily P&L Reporting
features.description: Generates daily profit-and-loss reports to give operators real-time visibility into restaurant performance.

features.title: Food Cost Control
features.description: Tracks food costs and usage across locations to help operators control margins.

features.title: Recipe Cost Calculations
features.description: Calculates recipe costs to support menu pricing decisions.

features.title: Menu Engineering & Analysis
features.description: Analyzes menu item profitability metrics to support menu engineering decisions.

features.title: Bill Payment Automation
features.description: Automates bill payment workflows for restaurant accounts payable.

features.title: Inventory Management
features.description: Tracks inventory and food usage across locations.

features.title: Multi-Location Transfer Management
features.description: Manages internal inventory transfers between locations for multi-unit restaurant groups.

pros: Processes invoices automatically, reducing manual back-office paperwork
Provides daily (not just monthly) P&L reporting for faster decision-making
Integrates with POS systems (Square, 7shifts, SpotOn), accounting software (Sage), and food distributors (Gordon Food Service, BEK)
Reports 97% customer satisfaction and a 4.5-star rating on Capterra
No long-term contracts; plans can be canceled anytime
cons: Pricing starts at $350/month per location, which can add up for multi-unit operators
Toast POS integration requires an additional $50/month per location fee
Liquor management (Freepour) is a paid add-on rather than included by default
No free trial is advertised on the pricing page

faqs.question: How much does MarginEdge cost?
faqs.answer: MarginEdge starts at $350 per month per location, with a $500/month per location bundle available that adds the Freepour liquor management feature.

faqs.question: Does MarginEdge integrate with my POS?
faqs.answer: MarginEdge integrates with POS systems including Square, 7shifts, and SpotOn.

faqs.question: Does MarginEdge offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: MarginEdge's pricing page does not list a free trial; plans instead include unlimited invoice processing, unlimited U.S. bill payments, and 1:1 training with no contracts.

faqs.question: How many restaurants use MarginEdge?
faqs.answer: MarginEdge reports serving more than 11,000 clients and has processed over 5.0 million invoices.

faqs.question: Where is MarginEdge based?
faqs.answer: MarginEdge is headquartered in Arlington, VA.

use_cases.title: Independent restaurant back-office automation
use_cases.description: Single-location restaurant operators use MarginEdge to automate invoice processing and get daily P&L visibility instead of manual bookkeeping.

use_cases.title: Multi-unit food cost control
use_cases.description: Multi-location restaurant groups use MarginEdge's transfer management and food cost tracking to standardize costs across units.

use_cases.title: Restaurant accounting firm support
use_cases.description: Accounting professionals serving restaurant clients use MarginEdge's automated invoice and bill pay tools, with special pricing available for accounting firms.

features: Freepour smart scale for liquor counting

long_form_blocks.heading: What is MarginEdge?
paragraphs: MarginEdge is restaurant back-office software that automates invoice processing, food cost tracking, and daily profit-and-loss reporting for restaurant operators.
The platform has processed more than 5.0 million invoices and reports serving over 11,000 restaurant clients, with pricing starting at $350 per month per location.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who MarginEdge is for
paragraphs: MarginEdge is built for independent restaurant owners, multi-unit restaurant groups, and accounting professionals who manage restaurant finances and need automated invoice processing and daily cost visibility.

enrichment.short_description: Cloud-based restaurant inventory management platform for purchasing, recipe costing, and COGS automation.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: MarketMan offers cloud-based restaurant inventory, purchasing, and recipe costing software starting at $199/month, used by 15,000+ restaurants.

features.title: Inventory Management
features.description: Provides real-time, web and mobile inventory management for restaurants.

features.title: Purchase Order Automation
features.description: Enables purchase order placement and receiving directly through the platform.

features.title: Price Tracking & Alerts
features.description: Tracks vendor pricing and sends alerts on price changes.

features.title: Recipe Costing
features.description: Calculates recipe costs and profitability analysis.

features.title: Waste Tracking
features.description: Tracks food waste as part of inventory management on the Growth plan and above.

features.title: AI-Powered Ordering
features.description: Uses AI-powered automation and predictive analytics for demand forecasting and ordering recommendations on the Enterprise plan.

features.title: Vendor Management
features.description: Automates vendor management workflows on the Growth plan and above.

features.title: COGS Automation
features.description: Automatically calculates cost of goods sold (COGS).

pros: Used by more than 15,000 restaurants worldwide
Offers dedicated iOS and Android mobile apps alongside the web app
Integrates with major POS platforms (Lightspeed, TouchBistro, Toast, Square), accounting software (QuickBooks, Xero), and distributors (US Foods, Sysco, PFG)
Includes AI-powered recipe creation and AI ordering on higher-tier plans
Customers report typical 3-5% COGS reduction and 100+ hours saved monthly
cons: Starter plan limits invoice scanning to 50 scans/month
AI ordering and some vendor integrations are reserved for the custom-priced Enterprise tier
No free trial is advertised on the pricing page
Enterprise pricing is custom and not published

faqs.question: How much does MarketMan cost?
faqs.answer: MarketMan's Starter plan is $199/month, Growth is $249/month, and Enterprise pricing is custom.

faqs.question: Does MarketMan have a mobile app?
faqs.answer: Yes, MarketMan offers a web application plus iOS and Android mobile apps.

faqs.question: What POS systems does MarketMan integrate with?
faqs.answer: MarketMan integrates with POS platforms including Lightspeed, TouchBistro, Toast, and Square.

faqs.question: Does MarketMan use AI?
faqs.answer: MarketMan includes AI-powered automation such as unlimited AI-powered recipe creation on the Growth plan and AI ordering with predictive analytics on the Enterprise plan.

faqs.question: How many restaurants use MarketMan?
faqs.answer: MarketMan reports being used by more than 15,000 restaurants worldwide.

use_cases.title: Single-location inventory control
use_cases.description: Independent restaurants use the Starter plan for purchase order placement, price tracking, and live inventory management.

use_cases.title: Recipe costing and waste reduction
use_cases.description: Restaurants on the Growth plan use automatic COGS calculation, waste tracking, and AI-powered recipe creation to control food costs.

use_cases.title: Multi-vendor enterprise purchasing
use_cases.description: Large multi-unit chains use the Enterprise plan's AI ordering and multiple vendor integrations to manage purchasing at scale.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is MarketMan?
paragraphs: MarketMan is a cloud-based restaurant management platform for inventory control, purchasing, expense tracking, and recipe costing.
The platform is used by more than 15,000 restaurants worldwide, with plans starting at $199/month for the Starter tier.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who MarketMan is for
paragraphs: MarketMan serves quick-service restaurants, full-service dining establishments, cafes, bars, and ghost kitchens, from single-location operators to large multi-unit chains.

enrichment.short_description: Cloud-based restaurant accounting, inventory, and workforce management platform used by more than 52,000 restaurants.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Restaurant365 provides cloud accounting, inventory, and workforce management for restaurants, with custom pricing and R365 AI tools.

features.title: Accounting & AP Automation
features.description: Provides accounts payable automation, banking, financial reporting, budgeting, and bookkeeping tools.

features.title: Inventory & Purchasing
features.description: Manages inventory, recipes, purchasing, receiving, and cash management.

features.title: Workforce Scheduling
features.description: Offers scheduling, sales forecasting, employee training, and time/attendance tracking.

features.title: Payroll & HR
features.description: Handles hiring, onboarding, HR, and payroll processing.

features.title: R365 AI
features.description: An AI intelligence engine described by Restaurant365 as built on the full restaurant P&L, with capabilities like automated schedule generation, recipe integration, and expense variance detection.

features.title: Modular Platform
features.description: Accounting, Inventory & Purchasing, Workforce Management, and Payroll & HR modules can be selected individually.

pros: Used by more than 52,000 restaurants
Supports English and Spanish
Reports claimed benefits including 50% reduction in accounting time and 5% lower food costs
Modular structure lets operators adopt only the modules they need
Includes R365 AI for schedule generation and expense variance detection
cons: Pricing is entirely custom/quote-based and not published
No free trial is mentioned; a demo must be requested
Requires contacting sales for a custom quote before pricing is known

faqs.question: How much does Restaurant365 cost?
faqs.answer: Restaurant365 uses custom pricing; the company directs prospective customers to request a custom quote from its sales team rather than publishing plan prices.

faqs.question: What does Restaurant365 include?
faqs.answer: Restaurant365 offers modular Accounting, Inventory & Purchasing, Workforce Management, and Payroll & HR modules that can be selected individually.

faqs.question: How many restaurants use Restaurant365?
faqs.answer: Restaurant365 states that more than 52,000 restaurants use the platform.

faqs.question: What languages does Restaurant365 support?
faqs.answer: Restaurant365 supports English and Spanish.

faqs.question: Where is Restaurant365 headquartered?
faqs.answer: Restaurant365 lists offices in Irvine, CA; Austin, TX; Mexico City; Akron, OH; and Nablus, Palestine.

use_cases.title: Restaurant accounting automation
use_cases.description: Restaurant groups use Restaurant365's accounting module for AP automation, budgeting, and financial reporting to reduce manual bookkeeping.

use_cases.title: Franchise operations management
use_cases.description: Franchise brands use Restaurant365's inventory, purchasing, and workforce modules to standardize operations across locations.

use_cases.title: Restaurant accounting firm services
use_cases.description: Accounting firms and bookkeepers serving restaurant clients use Restaurant365's accounting and reporting tools.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Restaurant365?
paragraphs: Restaurant365 is a cloud-based management system offering integrated accounting, inventory, workforce scheduling, and payroll for restaurants.
The platform reports more than 52,000 restaurant users and offers modular Accounting, Inventory & Purchasing, Workforce Management, and Payroll & HR products with custom pricing.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Restaurant365 is for
paragraphs: Restaurant365 serves small business restaurants, multi-location restaurant groups, franchise brands, and accounting firms across restaurant styles including QSR, casual dining, fast casual, fine dining, coffee shops, and pizza concepts.

enrichment.short_description: Back-office platform for hospitality businesses covering purchasing, inventory, recipe costing, and AP automation.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Craftable is back-office software for restaurants and hotels, unifying purchasing, inventory, recipe costing, and AP automation.

features.title: Intelligent Ordering
features.description: Manages vendor orders and flags price discrepancies during purchasing.

features.title: Inventory & Recipe Management
features.description: Tracks inventory in real time and connects it to recipes and theoretical usage.

features.title: AP Automation
features.description: Uses AI-powered invoice processing with PO matching and three-way reconciliation.

features.title: Daily Actionable Insights
features.description: Provides a unified dashboard combining food cost, labor, and purchasing data.

features.title: Operator AI
features.description: Surfaces daily ranked insights across operations.

features.title: Invoice AI
features.description: Automates scanning and processing of vendor invoices.

features.title: Crafty AI Assistant
features.description: In-app AI assistant for answering recipe profitability questions.

pros: Combines purchasing, inventory, recipe costing, and AP automation in one platform
Reports 1,000+ POS, EDI, and API integrations
SOC 2 compliant with SSO support
Used by 10,000+ operators including bartaco, Jim's Steakout, PopStroke, and Sugarfire Smokehouse
Includes AI-driven invoice processing and operational insights
cons: No public pricing; requires a demo/sales conversation
No mobile app mentioned on the official site
Supported languages are not documented publicly

faqs.question: Does Craftable publish pricing online?
faqs.answer: No, Craftable does not display pricing publicly; it uses a demo-based sales process.

faqs.question: What types of businesses use Craftable?
faqs.answer: Full-service and limited-service restaurants, fast-casual chains, hotels, resorts, and entertainment venues.

faqs.question: Does Craftable offer AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, including Operator AI for insights, Invoice AI for automated invoice processing, and Crafty, an in-app AI assistant.

faqs.question: Is Craftable SOC 2 compliant?
faqs.answer: Yes, Craftable states it is SOC 2 compliant and supports SSO.

faqs.question: How many integrations does Craftable support?
faqs.answer: Craftable states it has 1,000+ POS, EDI, and API integrations.

use_cases.title: Multi-location restaurant purchasing
use_cases.description: Centralizes vendor ordering and flags price discrepancies across locations.

use_cases.title: Recipe cost control
use_cases.description: Connects real-time inventory data to recipes to track theoretical usage and food cost.
use_cases.audience: Restaurant and hotel kitchen operations teams

use_cases.title: Invoice and AP automation
use_cases.description: Automates invoice scanning, PO matching, and reconciliation to reduce manual AP work.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Craftable?
paragraphs: Craftable is back-office software for hospitality businesses that consolidates purchasing, inventory, accounts payable, and financial reporting into a single platform.

The platform is operated by FNBTech, Inc. and is used by full-service and limited-service restaurants, fast-casual chains, hotels, resorts, and entertainment venues, with named customers including bartaco, Jim's Steakout, PopStroke, and Sugarfire Smokehouse.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Craftable is for
paragraphs: Craftable targets multi-unit and enterprise hospitality operators that need to unify purchasing, inventory, recipe costing, and invoice processing across locations.

enrichment.short_description: Multi-site F&B operations platform for recipe management, inventory, purchasing, and menu engineering.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Apicbase is a multi-site F&B management platform for recipe standardization, inventory, purchasing, and menu engineering.

features.title: Recipe Management
features.description: Standardizes recipes with automatic dietary data completion and allergen compliance.

features.title: Menu Engineering
features.description: Analyzes profitability using sales data and AI-driven forecasting.

features.title: Inventory Management
features.description: Tracks inventory in real time across kitchens and outlets, including barcode and voice-based counting.

features.title: Purchasing & Receiving
features.description: Generates smart order suggestions and handles invoice matching and spend controls.

features.title: Group-wide Analytics
features.description: Provides group-wide reporting and demand forecasting.

features.title: Compliance Tools
features.description: Supports HACCP, traceability, allergen communication, and audit-ready reporting.

features.title: Sustainability Tracking
features.description: Tracks carbon, waste, water, and land usage metrics.

pros: Covers recipe management, purchasing, inventory, and compliance in one platform
Supports multiple languages: English, Dutch, French, and German
Includes a mobile app on the Growth plan and higher
Used across 1,000+ outlets and by named customers including Vermaat, Loetje, citizenM, and Compass Group
Tiered plans (Growth, Professional, Enterprise) scale features with business size
cons: No specific dollar pricing published; requires contacting sales
Monthly billing carries an additional 15% fee versus annual billing
Growth plan requires a minimum of 5 outlets
Advanced features like demand forecasting and SSO are reserved for higher-tier plans

faqs.question: Does Apicbase publish its pricing?
faqs.answer: Apicbase does not list specific dollar prices publicly; annual billing is standard, with a 15% additional fee for monthly billing.

faqs.question: What is the minimum plan size for Apicbase?
faqs.answer: The Growth plan starts from 5 outlets.

faqs.question: What languages does Apicbase support?
faqs.answer: Apicbase's site is available in English, Dutch, French, and German.

faqs.question: Where is Apicbase headquartered?
faqs.answer: Apicbase is headquartered in Belgium, with an extensive remote team.

faqs.question: Does Apicbase offer a mobile app?
faqs.answer: Yes, a mobile app is included starting with the Growth plan.

use_cases.title: Multi-site recipe standardization
use_cases.description: Standardizes recipes and automates dietary and allergen data across many locations.

use_cases.title: Menu profitability analysis
use_cases.description: Analyzes sales data to optimize menu pricing and profitability with AI-driven forecasting.

use_cases.title: Group-wide inventory and purchasing
use_cases.description: Centralizes inventory counting and purchasing with order suggestions and invoice matching across outlets.
use_cases.audience: Operations and procurement teams at large F&B groups

use_cases.title: Compliance and sustainability reporting
use_cases.description: Tracks HACCP compliance, traceability, and environmental metrics like carbon and waste.
use_cases.audience: Compliance and sustainability teams at foodservice companies

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Apicbase?
paragraphs: Apicbase is a multi-site food and beverage operations platform that centralizes recipe management, purchasing, inventory, and cost management for distributed restaurant, catering, and hotel businesses.
The company is headquartered in Belgium and serves 1,000+ outlets globally, with named customers including Vermaat, Loetje, citizenM, and Compass Group.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Apicbase is for
paragraphs: Apicbase is built for multi-unit and enterprise foodservice operators that need standardized recipes, group-wide inventory and purchasing control, and compliance reporting across many locations.

enrichment.short_description: Recipe management and food costing software that helps culinary teams organize recipes, calculate margins, and train kitchen staff.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Meez is a recipe management and food costing platform for chefs and multi-unit restaurant teams, starting at $19/month.

features.title: Recipe Organization
features.description: Searchable, version-controlled recipe hub with support for photos and videos.

features.title: Automatic Food Costing
features.description: Calculates food costs automatically, accounting for yields, unit of measure, and prep loss.

features.title: Recipe Scaling & Conversions
features.description: One-click recipe scaling with automatic unit conversions.

features.title: Kitchen Training
features.description: Visual, step-by-step recipe training designed for consistent back-of-house onboarding.

features.title: Menu Engineering
features.description: Real-time profitability analysis and scenario modeling for menu items.

features.title: Allergen Management
features.description: Automatically tags allergens in every recipe.

features.title: Nutrition Labeling
features.description: Built-in database of 2,500+ ingredients for nutrition label generation.

features.title: AI Recipe Import
features.description: Uses AI to capture and digitize recipes with built-in yields.

pros: Tiered pricing starting at an accessible rate for individual chefs
Native integrations with Restaurant365, MarketMan, MarginEdge, Ottimate, and Over Easy Office
AI-assisted recipe import reduces manual data entry
Used by 35,000+ culinary professionals including named brands like José Andrés Group and True Food Kitchen
Covers the full workflow from recipe creation to menu engineering and allergen compliance
cons: Higher-tier plans (Premium, Enterprise) require custom quotes or significant monthly spend
Backoffice system connections and invoice processing are limited to higher-priced plans
Add-ons like extra cost feeds and data warehouse sync carry additional monthly fees

faqs.question: How much does Meez cost?
faqs.answer: Meez's Starter plan starts at $19/month billed annually (or $24/month billed monthly); Pro and Premium plans cost more and include additional devices and features, while Enterprise pricing is custom.

faqs.question: Does Meez offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Meez plans can be tried for free before committing to a paid subscription.

faqs.question: Does Meez integrate with other restaurant software?
faqs.answer: Yes, Meez has native integrations with Restaurant365, MarketMan, MarginEdge, Ottimate, and Over Easy Office.

faqs.question: Can Meez calculate food costs automatically?
faqs.answer: Yes, Meez automatically calculates food costs based on yields, unit of measure, and prep loss.

faqs.question: Does Meez support allergen and nutrition labeling?
faqs.answer: Yes, Meez can automatically tag allergens and includes a database of 2,500+ ingredients for nutrition labeling, available on higher-tier plans.

use_cases.title: Independent chef recipe organization
use_cases.description: Individual chefs use the Starter plan to organize recipes and calculate basic food costs.

use_cases.title: Single-kitchen training and costing
use_cases.description: Single kitchen teams use Meez for staff training, prep standardization, and food costing.

use_cases.title: Multi-location menu engineering
use_cases.description: Hospitality groups use Meez for menu engineering, nutrition labeling, and allergen compliance across multiple locations.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Meez?
paragraphs: Meez is a recipe management and food costing platform built for culinary teams. It gives chefs and operators a centralized, searchable hub for recipes, complete with automatic food cost calculations that account for yields, unit of measure, and prep loss.

Beyond recipe storage, Meez supports kitchen training, menu engineering, allergen tagging, and nutrition labeling, with native integrations to accounting and inventory platforms like Restaurant365, MarketMan, and MarginEdge.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Meez is for
paragraphs: Meez serves everyone from independent chefs organizing their own recipes to multi-unit hospitality groups managing menu engineering, nutrition compliance, and allergen tracking across dozens of locations.

enrichment.short_description: Culinary resource planning platform for recipe management, menu planning, and food cost insights used by chefs and foodservice operators.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Galley is a culinary resource planning platform offering recipe management, menu planning, and real-time food cost insights, with a free trial.

features.title: Recipe Management & Scaling
features.description: Centralized recipe management with tools to scale recipes for different production needs.

features.title: Menu Planning Optimization
features.description: Tools to plan and optimize menus based on cost and operational data.

features.title: Real-Time Cost Insights
features.description: Provides real-time visibility into food costs to support financial accuracy.

features.title: Allergen Tracking Automation
features.description: Automates allergen tracking across recipes and menus.

features.title: Kitchen Workflow Optimization
features.description: Supports optimization of kitchen workflows for operational efficiency.

pros: Free trial available to test the platform before purchase
Rated 4.8 out of 5 on Capterra
Used to create 150,000+ recipes and support 5 million+ meals served across customers
Reported to save operators 45+ hours per week
Serves multiple roles including chefs, foodservice operators, and dietitians
cons: Pricing details are not published on the website and require contacting sales
Platform support beyond web (e.g. iOS/Android) is not documented on the site
Founding year, headquarters, and detailed integrations are not disclosed on the site

faqs.question: Does Galley offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Galley's website states you can "Try it today for free!"

faqs.question: How much does Galley cost?
faqs.answer: Galley does not publish pricing on its website; pricing requires contacting the company directly.

faqs.question: What is Galley used for?
faqs.answer: Galley is used for recipe management, menu planning, real-time food cost insights, and allergen tracking in commercial kitchens.

faqs.question: Who uses Galley?
faqs.answer: Galley is used by chefs, foodservice operators, and dietitians.

faqs.question: What rating does Galley have on Capterra?
faqs.answer: Galley has a 4.8 out of 5 rating on Capterra, according to its website.

use_cases.title: Recipe standardization and scaling
use_cases.description: Culinary teams use Galley to manage and scale recipes consistently across a kitchen operation.

use_cases.title: Menu cost planning
use_cases.description: Foodservice operators use Galley to plan menus with real-time cost insights.

use_cases.title: Allergen and nutrition tracking
use_cases.description: Dietitians and operators use Galley's allergen tracking automation to manage compliance.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Galley?
paragraphs: Galley describes itself as "culinary resource planning for every kitchen," a platform combining recipe management, menu planning, and real-time food cost insights for commercial kitchens.
The platform reports supporting the creation of 150,000+ recipes and 5 million+ meals served across its customer base, with a free trial available to new users.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Galley is for
paragraphs: Galley is built for chefs, foodservice operators, and dietitians who need to manage recipes, plan menus, and track food costs and allergens in a centralized system.
